findings.title: No executeStage3 boundary test covers the rerank threshold before MPAB chunk collapse
findings.summary: The new boundary tests call applyCrossEncoderReranking directly, so they prove 3 raw rows skip and 4 raw rows apply. They do not exercise executeStage3's full order, where reranking happens before MPAB collapse. A candidate set with four chunk rows for fewer than four final parent documents can still pass the new threshold and invoke the reranker, which is the exact cost/benefit edge this packet intended to tune.

findings.title: The minimum rerank gate counts raw rows, not distinct documents after chunk collapse
findings.summary: The inline rationale says reranking 2-3 docs is not worth it, but the gate uses results.length before chunk rows are grouped by parent and collapsed. This can still rerank 4+ chunk rows that represent only 1-3 parent documents. The behavior may be acceptable because Stage 3 intentionally scores raw chunks first, but it is a correctness/rationale mismatch worth resolving explicitly.

findings.title: Local reranker candidate-cap test does not exercise the production cap path
findings.summary: The test named MAX_RERANK_CANDIDATES enforcement relies on model-load fallback and then manually verifies Array.slice(0, 50). It does not install a mock model/context that reaches the production loop over rerankCandidates.slice(0, rerankCount), so the cap can regress without this test failing.

rejectedFindings.reason: Rejected by user evidence rule because this pass must not carry over spec-doc-only findings from review/review-report.md.
